findings.description: Nested <main> landmark elements - ensure the document has at most one main landmark at top level
findings.evidence: <main> at line 27 contains another <main> at line 37. Only one <main> landmark is valid per document.

findings.description: The PDF agenda is formatted as a simple list with dotted leader lines connecting topics to speakers. The HTML converts this into a formal table with headers (Time, Topic, Speaker), borders, and separate rows for sub-items like 'Confirm local data source contacts', 'Model schematic', and 'Inflow development'. In the PDF, these sub-items are grouped under their parent time slots, but in the HTML they appear as separate rows with empty Time and Speaker cells.
findings.evidence: PDF: Sub-items like 'Confirm local data source contacts' appear directly under '1:10 - 2:00' as part of the same entry. HTML: These sub-items are rendered as separate table rows with blank Time and Speaker columns, which could suggest they are distinct agenda items rather than sub-points.

findings.description: The HTML fabricates table column headers (Time, Topic, Speaker) that do not exist in the source PDF. The PDF presents the agenda as a simple list, not a table with labeled columns.
findings.evidence: HTML contains <thead> with <th>Time</th>, <th>Topic</th>, <th>Speaker</th>. The PDF has no such column headers — it presents the agenda as formatted text lines with dotted leaders.

findings.description: The sub-items 'Confirm local data source contacts', 'Model schematic', and 'Inflow development' are separated into their own rows with empty Time and Speaker cells. While this is a reasonable structural interpretation, it fragments what are clearly sub-bullets of their parent agenda items in the PDF into standalone rows, which could be misleading.
findings.evidence: PDF shows 'Confirm local data source contacts' as a sub-item under the 1:10-2:00 slot, and 'Model schematic' / 'Inflow development' as sub-items under 2:20-3:10. HTML puts them in separate <tr> elements with empty <td> cells.

findings.description: The HTML uses h2 for 'Stakeholder Meeting #2' which is actually a subtitle/part of the document title in the PDF, not a separate section heading. This is a minor structural choice but acceptable.
findings.evidence: PDF shows 'French Broad Hydrologic Model' and 'Stakeholder Meeting #2' as a combined title block. HTML splits them into h1 and h2.
